title: forward_to_provider_raw_reqwest: unused _api_key parameter and misleading name
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

Dimension: Documentation & Intent / Cognitive Readability

Location: src/proxy.rs line 1420-1443 (fn forward_to_provider_raw_reqwest).

Violation: The function takes an `_api_key: &str` parameter that is IGNORED (named with underscore prefix), but the name 'forward_to_provider_raw_reqwest' implies it would use the api_key. The actual auth header construction has been moved to the caller (proxy_chat_completions passes custom_headers vec that already contains the Authorization from adapter.get_headers). The unused api_key parameter is a vestigial API contract that:
  (a) Mis-leads the reader.
  (b) Prevents refactoring — every call site still passes api_key for no reason.
  (c) Violates YAGNI — there is no plan to use it.

Additionally the function name 'forward_to_provider_raw_reqwest' is non-descriptive — the '_raw_reqwest' suffix is a synonym for the previous 'forward_to_provider' which also uses reqwest. The real difference is that this variant returns the raw reqwest::Response without converting it. A better name is 'send_to_provider' or 'send_raw'.

Mandated Refactor:
  1. Delete the `_api_key: &str` parameter from forward_to_provider_raw_reqwest. Update all callers.
  2. Rename to `send_raw_request(client, provider, path, body, headers) -> Result<reqwest::Response, ProxyError>`.
  3. forward_to_provider (which returns the converted axum Response) should internally call send_raw_request and then convert_response. DRY the two functions that share the URL construction and provider.headers loop.
